title: Bridge Essay: My avatar moves with me, so I am the one acting: Avatar responsiveness supports implicit sense of agency in virtual reality.
concept: Agency as inference — the 'I' as nested prediction error minimization, not homunculus.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nested Agency** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Agency as inference — the 'I' as nested prediction error minimization, not homunculus.

**My avatar moves with me, so I am the one acting: Avatar responsiveness supports implicit sense of agency in virtual reality.** - PloS one (2026). DOI: 10.1371/journal.pone.0351839. PMID: 42330024. Virtual reality (VR) allows users to interact with computer-generated environments in a controlled yet immersive manner. One of its most relevant features is its ability to induce virtual embodiment, the process of becoming rooted in a virtual body. A key component of this experience is the sense of agency over the action-outcomes produced in the virtual environment. However, to date, no study has jointly examined explicit and implicit senses of agency within a VR paradigm in which actions lead
